steps.title: Identify the evaluative language
steps.content: The key signal is “thus capitalizing on” combined with the description of the source as “vast but hitherto relatively underutilized.” This language judges the value and importance of the approach—it evaluates significance rather than specifying applications or explaining causes.

steps.title: Match the function to the choices
steps.content:
Choice C correctly identifies this as “commentary on the significance”—the underlined portion evaluates how important and valuable this methodological choice is.

The other choices describe different functions:
  • A specifies concrete applications that don't appear here
  • B shifts focus from the approach to the findings themselves
  • D seeks causal explanation rather than assessment of value
.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
